steps.title: Set up vertex form using the maximum
steps.content:
The ball reaches its maximum height of 200 ft at $t = 3$ s, so the vertex is $(3, 200)$. Vertex form:
$$f(t) = a(t - 3)^2 + 200$$

steps.title: Solve for the leading coefficient using the initial condition
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $f(t) = -16(t + 200)^2 - 3$: Places the vertex at $(-200, -3)$, which is completely wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — $f(t) = -16(t - 200)^2 + 3$: Places the vertex at $(200, 3)$; the maximum height would be only 3 ft, not 200. ✗
  • Choice C — $f(t) = -16(t + 3)^2 - 200$: Places the vertex at $(-3, -200)$; the maximum height would be $-200$ ft, which is impossible. ✗
  • Choice D — $f(t) = -16(t - 3)^2 + 200$: Vertex at $(3, 200)$, initial height $f(0) = 56$. Both conditions satisfied. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
